steps.title: Translate the phrase into an expression
steps.content: "7 times a number x" is $7x$. "5 more than" means add 5, giving $7x + 5$.

steps.title: Set equal to 75
steps.content: The full statement "is equal to 75" gives: $$7x + 5 = 75$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• A: $(5)(7)x = 75$ simplifies to $35x = 75$ - this is 35 times x, not 5 more than 7 times x. Eliminate A.
  • B: $7x = 75 + 5$ puts the 5 on the wrong side, giving $7x = 80$. Eliminate B.
  • C: $5x + 7 = 75$ swaps the roles of 5 and 7 - this is "7 more than 5 times x." Eliminate C.
  • D: $7x + 5 = 75$ is exactly "5 more than 7 times x equals 75." D is correct.
